comment: Exact label match: EDAS Paper and EKAW Paper are both document subclasses representing papers.
Exact label match: both denote review documents and are subclasses of Document.
Exact label match and consistent structural role as superclass of papers and reviews.
Exact label match with compatible use as superclass for conference participants and authors/reviewers.
Exact label match; both model a conference event edition.
Exact label match; both are scientific event types distinct from conference and session.
Exact label match; both denote a presenting participant role.
Near-exact lexical match differing only by separator/casing; both denote chair of a session.
TPCMember in EDAS closely corresponds to Program Committee member in EKAW; likely same conference role but naming differs.
EDAS Author is modeled as a person related to a paper; EKAW Paper_Author is a person authoring a paper. Strong semantic correspondence.
EDAS Reviewer denotes an actual reviewer of some paper, narrower than EKAW Possible_Reviewer which includes potential reviewers.
Near-exact lexical match and same intended meaning as a session belonging to a conference.
EDAS Topic corresponds closely to EKAW Research_Topic as subject matter covered by papers/events.
Lexical variant due to American/British spelling; both denote organizations.
Place and Location are strong semantic equivalents in the event-location context.
Near-exact lexical match; both represent social events associated with conferences.
Related but not equivalent: EDAS Workshop is an academic event, while EKAW Workshop_Session is a session subtype.
Near-exact lexical match and same acceptance status semantics.
Near-exact lexical match and same rejection status semantics.
Both properties link papers/events to topics, though domain/range are expressed differently.
Inverse topic-linking properties with aligned meaning.
Both link an event to its place/location.
Inverse location properties with matching semantics.
Weak relation only; no strong EKAW attendee property is present. Included as low-confidence relatedness would normally be omitted.
Very weak structural similarity only; EDAS hasMember links conference to person, EKAW hasPart links event to event. Not equivalent.
Both properties link reviewers to papers they review.
Inverse reviewer assignment properties with corresponding meaning.
EDAS hasRelatedPaper links Author to Paper; EKAW authorOf links Person to Document. Close but not exact because EKAW is broader.
Both link document/paper to authoring person, but EKAW writtenBy has broader domain Document.
EDAS Programme as a document is close to EKAW Programme_Brochure, though brochure may be a more specific publication artifact.
Conference dinner and conference banquet are highly similar social meal events.
Excursion closely corresponds to conference trip as a social event.
